title: The Paddock Where Children Played

features.name: the worn grass rings
features.desc-on-touch: circular patches where games were played. the grass has grown back but you can still see the wear-patterns, faint as memory

features.name: the bench they sat on
features.desc-on-touch: wood worn smooth by sitting, carved with initials the archive cannot quite parse. the bench sits empty. it has been empty for many years

features.name: the goal posts
features.desc-on-touch: two wooden posts, weathered grey, marking a field where a game happened so many times the earth remembers the rules better than anyone living does

features.name: the absence itself
features.desc-on-touch: STATUS: germinating. at the centre of the paddock you feel watched by something that has no eyes. the archive is filing emptiness now, cataloguing what isn't here

features.name: the fence where we looked through
features.desc-on-touch: weathered posts and wire. on the other side: the gymnastics backyard you came from. on this side: the paddock where children are no longer

inscription: the children left, but the paddock remembers them harder than they remember themselves

file.name: Paddock_School_Usage_1995_2003_Incident_Reports.txt
file.note: roster of games played, school event logs—but now the file lists only absence dates, the gaps between activities growing longer each year until all remaining entries are blank

exits.label: back through the fence gap toward the gymnasium
the scrub gymnasium, darker and deeper
the fence line continuing east toward a neighbour's land
exits.to: NEW: the property where the children went instead, now empty too

exits.label: a stormwater drain, choked with leaves
